intro: Polytoca barbata, also known as Polytoca, is a medicinal plant native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America, belonging to the Gentianaceae family. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Polytoca barbata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Polytoca is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion to treat respiratory conditions, and is also used in the production of cough syrups and expectorants.
